items.title: Marine Weather Statement issued March 31 at 7:24AM EDT by NWS Detroit/Pontiac MI
items.summary:
The areas affected include...
Lake Huron from Port Sanilac to Port Huron 5NM Off Shore to
US/Canadian border...
Port Sanilac to Port Huron MI...

At 724 AM EDT, Doppler radar indicated a strong thunderstorm, capable
of producing winds up to 30 knots and small hail. This thunderstorm
was located near Lexington, moving east at 55 knots.

The strong thunderstorm will remain over mainly open waters.
items.area: Port Sanilac to Port Huron MI; Lake Huron from Port Sanilac to Port Huron 5NM Off Shore to US/Canadian border

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 31 at 5:40AM CDT by NWS Quad Cities IA IL
items.summary:
At 540 AM CDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm over
Annawan, or 10 miles north of Kewanee, moving east at 40 mph.

HAZARD...Nickel size hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Minor damage to outdoor objects is possible.

This strong thunderstorm will be near...
Sheffield around 550 AM CDT.
Princeton around 610 AM CDT.
La Moille around 625 AM CDT.

Other locations impacted by this storm include Mineral, Seatonville,
Cherry, Dover, Coal Hollow, Zearing, Kasbeer, Manlius, Ohio,
items.area: Henry; Bureau

items.title: Winter Weather Advisory issued March 31 at 3:13AM PDT until April 2 at 11:00AM PDT by NWS Eureka CA
items.summary:
* WHAT...Snow expected. Total snow accumulations between 4 to 7
inches. Winds gusting as high as 35 mph.

* WHERE...Northern Trinity.

* WHEN...From 8 PM Wednesday to 11 AM PDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Highway 3 at Scott Mountain Pass will likely become slick
and hazardous. Plan on slippery road conditions.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Lighter snow accumulations up to 1 inch will
be possible as low as 2500 feet Thursday morning.
items.area: Northern Trinity

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 31 at 5:50AM EDT by NWS Baltimore MD/Washington DC
items.summary:
* WHAT...West to southwest winds 15 to 25 mph with occasional
gusts of 40 to 50 mph near and above 3000 feet.

* WHERE...In Maryland, Eastern Garrett and Extreme Western
Allegany Counties. In West Virginia, Western Grant and Western
Mineral Counties.

* WHEN...Until 9 AM EDT this morning.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds may blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.
items.area: Extreme Western Allegany; Eastern Garrett; Western Grant; Western Mineral

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 31 at 5:19AM EDT by NWS Wilmington NC
items.summary:
Patchy dense fog has developed across portions of Robeson County,
reducing visibility on area roadways to as low as one quarter
mile at times. This fog will affect the morning commute in some
areas before burning off by 9 AM.
items.area: Robeson

items.title: Winter Weather Advisory issued March 31 at 3:09AM MDT until April 1 at 12:00AM MDT by NWS Billings MT
items.summary:
* WHAT...Periods of snow. Snow accumulations between 2 and 6 inches,
greatest along the eastern slopes of the Crazy Mountains.

* WHERE...Southern Wheatland, Livingston Area, Melville Foothills,
and Northern Sweet Grass.

* WHEN...Until midnight MDT tonight.

* IMPACTS...Travel will be difficult due to the snow and poor
visibility. Roads during the day should remain wet, but icy or
slushy spots are possible early this morning and again this
evening. This includes I-90 from Big Timber to Livingst
items.area: Livingston Area; Northern Sweet Grass; Melville Foothills; Southern Wheatland

items.title: Winter Weather Advisory issued March 31 at 2:03AM PDT until April 2 at 8:00AM PDT by NWS Reno NV
items.summary:
* WHAT...Snow. Snow accumulations of 5 to 10 inches near the Sierra
crest including Donner Pass. Snow accumulations in the valleys of
1 to 4 inches with the higher amounts likely in the western shores
of Lake Tahoe. Winds gusting as high as 100 mph at the crest.
Sierra valley gusts of 45 to 55 mph. Wave heights on Lake Tahoe
from 1 to 4 feet.

* WHERE...Greater Lake Tahoe Area.

* WHEN...From 5 AM Wednesday to 8 AM PDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be very difficult. The hazardous conditio
items.area: Greater Lake Tahoe Area; Greater Lake Tahoe Area

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 31 at 4:59AM EDT by NWS Newport/Morehead City NC
items.summary:
The combination of afternoon relative humidity values around 40-45%
and very dry fine fuels will result in an increased risk for the
spread of wildfires this afternoon across interior Eastern North
Carolina.

Everyone is urged to properly dispose of cigarette butts,
matches, and any other flammable items in appropriate containers.
Additionally, exercise caution if operating any equipment that
could create sparks, and keep vehicles off of dry grass. Most dry
grasses, dead leaves, and other tree l
items.area: Martin; Pitt; Greene; Duplin; Lenoir

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 31 at 4:54AM EDT by NWS Wakefield VA
items.summary:
The combination of relative humidity values in the 35 to 45 percent
range, dry fine fuels, ongoing moderate to severe drought, and
wind gusts up to 20 mph will result in an increased risk for the
rapid spread of wildfires today across portions of interior
northeast North Carolina west of the Chowan River.

Everyone is urged to properly dispose of cigarette butts, matches,
and any other flammable items in appropriate containers. Additionally,
exercise caution if operating any equipment that could
items.area: Northampton; Hertford; Bertie

items.title: Rip Current Statement issued March 31 at 3:57AM EDT until April 3 at 2:00AM EDT by NWS Tallahassee FL
items.summary:
* WHAT...Dangerous rip currents expected.

* WHERE...Walton, Bay, and South-facing Gulf County Beaches.

* WHEN...From late tonight through late Thursday night.

* IMPACTS...Rip currents can sweep even the best swimmers away
from shore into deeper water.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...A prolonged period of high rip current risk
is expected Wednesday through this weekend. Despite the warm and
sunny weather, the surf will be dangerous for all levels of
swimmers, especially visitors.
items.area: South Walton; Coastal Bay; Coastal Gulf

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 31 at 3:50AM EDT by NWS Raleigh NC
items.summary:
The combination of low relative humidity, gusty winds, and very
dry fuels will lead to an increased risk of wildfires today
across central North Carolina.

Residents and visitors are urged to properly dispose of cigarette
butts, matches, and any other flammable items in appropriate
containers. Additionally, exercise caution if operating any
equipment that could create sparks, and keep vehicles off of dry
leaves and grass. Most dry grasses, dead leaves, and other tree
litter could easily ignite a

items.title: Fire Weather Watch issued March 31 at 1:37AM CDT until April 1 at 9:00PM CDT by NWS Lubbock TX
items.summary:
The National Weather Service in Lubbock has issued a Fire Weather
Watch, which is in effect from Wednesday afternoon through
Wednesday evening.

* Timing...12 PM CDT Wednesday until 9 PM CDT Wednesday.

* Wind...Southwest at 25 to 35 mph with gusts up to 50 mph.

* Humidity...As low as 17 percent.

* Fuels...Critically to extremely dry.

* Impacts...Any fires that develop can spread rapidly. Outdoor
burning is discouraged.
items.area: Parmer; Castro; Swisher; Briscoe; Bailey; Lamb; Hale; Floyd; Cochran; Hockley; Lubbock; Crosby; Yoakum; Terry; Lynn; Garza
